organizations.name: Dev Coop Meetup
organizations.why: Too many Meetups were about talking, I wanted one about doing.
organizations.how: A monthly Meetup, Slack team, and GitHub organization focused on hands on collaboration.  Attracted well over 100 developers.  Sponsored by JetBrains and GravityJack.

organizations.name: 3 Strand Code
organizations.why: I wanted to help others find joy and purpose in web development.
organizations.how: Free senior developer apprenticeships.  I helped folks get jobs and helped others advance their careers.

projects.name: Rubber Ducky
projects.why: Convinced LLMs were the new transistors, I set out to build the future of the developer experience from the ground up.
projects.how: An agentic developer tool. Local server powers CLI and UI with full system access (fs, browser, APIs). Framework-free build, model-agnostic (Anthropic, OpenAI, Azure, Ollama), pushing the limits of LLM-driven automation.
projects.impact: Organically adopted by 2,000+ engineers across 40+ Microsoft orgs. Became officially recommended tooling alongside GitHub Copilot and Claude Code.

projects.name: Open UI
projects.why: I wanted to eliminate the need to build UI frameworks by pushing design system primitives down into the web platform itself.
projects.how: W3C Community Group, cross enterprise. Analyze top UI design systems and frameworks, synthesize their needs into platform specs and ship in browsers. Members from Google, Apple, Salesforce, Mozilla, Microsoft, Shopify, Adobe, and more.
projects.impact: After I scaffolded the system and process, immediate success with memberships and adoption. Ongoing specifications and browser updates continue. Grew to lead efforts in WHATWG and CSS WG.

projects.name: Fluent UI React
projects.why: As Microsoft Teams' framework lead, I needed a design system and UI library which could unify Teams and M365 products.
projects.how: Created a cross-org cross-product design to code flow to support legacy, enable the future, and achieve >30% performance increase in the process.
projects.impact: Powers Microsoft Teams, OneDrive, SharePoint, Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and other products. 20k GitHub stars and used in the open source as well.

projects.name: Semantic UI React
projects.why: My team needed a React port of Semantic UI. The existing ports weren't up to standard, so I built one.
projects.how: Rewrote Semantic UI's components in React from scratch. Originally named Stardust, it was chosen by the Semantic Org to become the official React port.
projects.impact: GitHub top 30 project. 500k+ downloads/month. Adopted by Amazon Publishing, Netflix's Edge Developer Experience team, Netflix's FlameScope, and Microsoft Teams prototyping.

projects.name: Anny
projects.why: I wanted to understand artificial neural networks from first principles.
projects.how: An artificial neural network lib for Node.js and the browser.  A network state and training visualizer.

projects.name: SparrowBleu Photography
projects.why: SparrowBleu Photography's clients needed a dead simple gallery for reviewing and purchasing photos.
projects.how: A photographer's client proofing gallery for mobile and desktop.

projects.name: Bootstyle
projects.why: The Bootstrap customizer lacked some features I wanted.  I had an idea for an in-browser theme builder and wanted to give it a shot.
